title: IWMF Kari Howard Fund for Narrative Journalism 2026: Up to $5,000 Grant for Women and Nonbinary Journalists Worldwide
summary: The International Women’s Media Foundation (IWMF) has officially opened applications for the prestigious Kari Howard Fund for Narrative Journalism 2026, an important funding opportunity designed to support compelling, impactful, and deeply human-centered storytelling from women and nonbinary journalists around the world. This grant program honors the legacy of Kari Howard, an editor celebrated for championing stories that captured the emotional depth of everyday life while illuminating major social issues through powerful narrative journalism. The fund aims to empower journalists who are passionate about telling meaningful stories that connect audiences with real people, communities, and experiences. For journalists seeking financial support to pursue ambitious reporting projects, this opportunity provides funding of up to $5,000 to help bring important stories to life. About the Kari Howard Fund for Narrative Journalism The Kari Howard Fund was established by Kari Howard’s family and friends in partnership with the International Women’s Media Foundation (IWMF). The initiative reflects Kari Howard’s dedication to narrative journalism that combines investigative depth with emotional resonance. The grant supports print journalists and multimedia teams producing narrative-driven reporting projects published in English anywhere in the world. Projects funded through this program are expected to focus on immersive storytelling, meaningful reporting, and journalism that highlights critical issues through personal and compelling narratives. Grant Benefits Selected applicants will receive several important benefits, including: Grants averaging approximately $5,000 Financial support for reporting and production expenses International recognition through the IWMF network Opportunity to pursue impactful narrative journalism projects Support for both independent journalists and collaborative multimedia teams Increased visibility for underreported or deeply human-centered stories Who Can Apply? The opportunity is specifically designed for: Women journalists Nonbinary journalists Gender nonconforming journalists Applicants may apply as: Freelance journalists Staff journalists Print journalists leading multimedia reporting teams Eligibility Requirements To qualify for the Kari Howard Fund for Narrative Journalism 2026, applicants must meet the following criteria: Professional Experience Applicants must have: At least three (3) years of professional journalism experience Journalism as their primary profession Please note that internships do not count toward the required professional experience. Journalism Focus Applicants must be: Print journalists Or print journalists leading a multi-format or multimedia team Any multimedia components must support and supplement the primary print journalism project. Language Requirement Applicants must: Apply in English Publish their reporting project in English Stories may be published digitally and do not necessarily need to appear in traditional newspapers or magazines. Editorial Support Applicants must either: Demonstrate proof of interest from an editor, or Show a strong publication history in prominent media outlets Eligible Reporting Projects The IWMF welcomes narrative journalism projects covering important issues from anywhere in the world. Projects may include: Social justice reporting Human rights stories Investigative narratives Community-centered storytelling Cultural reporting Gender-focused reporting Political or humanitarian issues Environmental storytelling Longform journalism projects The focus should be on strong storytelling that creates emotional connection while addressing important issues. Project Timeline Successful applicants must: Complete all reporting and publication activities within six months of receiving the grant award Applicants should therefore submit realistic timelines and achievable reporting plans in their proposals. Application Deadline The deadline to apply for the IWMF Kari Howard Fund for Narrative Journalism 2026 is: July 12, 2026, at 11:59 PM EST Applicants are expected to receive decisions by early September 2026. Why This Opportunity Matters Narrative journalism remains one of the most powerful forms of storytelling in modern media. Through immersive reporting and emotionally engaging narratives, journalists can help audiences better understand complex issues affecting people worldwide. The Kari Howard Fund continues to preserve and promote this important journalistic tradition by supporting reporters dedicated to telling stories with empathy, depth, and authenticity. For women and nonbinary journalists looking to pursue meaningful reporting projects, this grant offers not only financial support but also recognition from one of the world’s leading organizations supporting women in media. Learn More Apply Interested applicants should prepare: A strong narrative journalism project proposal Evidence of professional journalism experience Proof of editorial interest or publication history A realistic reporting and publication timeline Any required supplementary application materials Applicants are encouraged to apply early to avoid last-minute technical issues. Click HERE to learn more and apply for the IWMF Kari Howard Fund for Narrative Journalism 2026. Explore more opportunities on OFY HERE . The post IWMF Kari Howard Fund for Narrative Journalism 2026: Up to $5,000 Grant for Women and Nonbinary Journalists Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Fully Funded Berrow Foundation Scholarships 2027 at the University of Oxford for Swiss Students | Study at Lincoln College, Oxford
summary: Applications are now open for the prestigious Berrow Foundation Scholarships 2027 at the University of Oxford, one of the world’s leading universities. These highly competitive fully funded scholarships provide exceptional Swiss students with the opportunity to pursue postgraduate studies at Oxford University as members of Lincoln College. The Berrow Foundation Scholarships are designed for Swiss nationals domiciled in Switzerland who wish to undertake graduate studies across a wide range of disciplines. In addition, the Berrow Foundation Lord Florey Scholarships specifically support postgraduate studies in medicine, chemistry, and biochemical sciences. The scholarships cover full tuition fees and provide a generous living stipend equivalent to the Rhodes Scholarship allowance, making this one of the most attractive international funding opportunities available to Swiss students. About the Berrow Foundation Scholarships The Berrow Foundation Scholarships are associated with Lincoln College at the University of Oxford and are awarded annually to outstanding Swiss graduates who demonstrate academic excellence, leadership potential, integrity, fellowship, and commitment to contributing positively to society. The scholarships are tenable only at Lincoln College, Oxford, and recipients are expected to actively participate in college and university life throughout their studies. The Berrow Foundation Scholarships support postgraduate programs such as: DPhil MSc MLitt MSt MPhil MJur BPhil Other eligible graduate programs at Oxford University Fields of Study Berrow Foundation Scholarships The general Berrow Foundation Scholarships support postgraduate study in virtually all academic disciplines available at Oxford University. Berrow Foundation Lord Florey Scholarships The Lord Florey Scholarships are specifically reserved for postgraduate studies in: Medicine Chemistry Biochemistry Related medical and biochemical sciences Applicants should note that research involving human embryos, cloning, or embryonic stem cells will not be funded under the Lord Florey Scholarships. Scholarship Benefits The Berrow Foundation Scholarships provide comprehensive financial support, including: Full Tuition Coverage The scholarships fully cover Oxford University combined course fees, including: University tuition fees Lincoln College fees Generous Living Stipend Recipients receive a living allowance equivalent to the Rhodes Scholarship stipend. For the 2025/26 academic year, this amounted to: £20,400 per annum This funding is intended to support accommodation, meals, transportation, and daily living expenses while studying in the United Kingdom. Additional Conference Funding (Lord Florey Scholars) Lord Florey Scholars may also receive: Up to £1,500 additional conference travel funding This can be used for presenting papers or posters at academic conferences. Scholarship Duration The scholarships are tenable for: Up to 3 academic years Lord Florey Scholars who require a fourth year to complete a DPhil may apply for additional financial support. Eligibility Criteria To qualify for the Berrow Foundation Scholarships, applicants must meet the following requirements: General Berrow Foundation Scholarships Applicants must: Be Swiss nationals Be domiciled in Switzerland Be graduates from: University of Berne University of Fribourg University of Geneva University of Lausanne University of Neuchâtel EPFL Candidates must also: Currently be studying at one of these institutions OR Be within five years of completing their first degree Applicants must not currently be studying for a degree at Oxford University. Lord Florey Scholarships Applicants must: Be Swiss nationals domiciled in Switzerland Be graduates from ANY Swiss university, including: ETH Zürich EPFL Other recognized Swiss universities Applicants must be within five years of graduation from their first degree. For medicine applicants, the five-year period is counted from obtaining the Federal Diploma in Medicine. Selection Criteria The Berrow Foundation evaluates applicants based on more than academic achievement alone. Candidates are assessed on qualities such as: Truth Courage Devotion to duty Fellowship Unselfishness Leadership potential Contribution to society Applicants should also demonstrate excellent written and spoken English proficiency before submitting an application. Scholarship Conditions Successful scholars are expected to: Undertake graduate study at Lincoln College, Oxford Contribute actively to college life Reside in Britain during the scholarship period Avoid paid employment without permission from the Foundation Maintain satisfactory academic progress Submit annual progress reports Acknowledge The Berrow Foundation in publications and public profiles The Foundation reserves the right to terminate the scholarship for unsatisfactory conduct or academic performance. Application Process The application process for the Berrow Foundation Scholarships involves multiple stages. Step 1: Apply for the Scholarship First Candidates must first submit an application to The Berrow Foundation before applying to Oxford University. Step 2: Submit Required Documents Applicants must provide: Completed online scholarship application form Two pieces of written work Two reference letters submitted directly by referees References should be emailed to: info@berrowfoundation.ch Step 3: Interview Stage Shortlisted applicants will be invited for interviews conducted in English in Geneva. Step 4: Apply to Oxford University Successful scholarship candidates must then submit their official graduate application to the University of Oxford and select Lincoln College as their preferred college choice. Important Dates Application Deadline The deadline for applications for October 2027 entry is: 28 August 2026 Interview Date Shortlisted candidates will be interviewed in Geneva on: 30 October 2026 Why You Should Apply The Berrow Foundation Scholarships represent one of the most prestigious fully funded postgraduate opportunities available to Swiss students. Studying at Oxford University offers: Access to world-class education Networking with global scholars and leaders Membership in the historic Lincoln College community Academic mentorship from leading researchers Career advancement opportunities International exposure and leadership development For ambitious Swiss students seeking postgraduate study at Oxford, this scholarship can be truly life-changing. Learn more and apply Interested applicants can apply through the Berrow Foundation Scholarships portal HERE . Click HERE to learn more about the Fully Funded Berrow Foundation Scholarships 2027. Explore more global fellowship opportunities on OFY here . The post Fully Funded Berrow Foundation Scholarships 2027 at the University of Oxford for Swiss Students | Study at Lincoln College, Oxford appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: WE-EMPOWER II Grant 2026: Up to €7,500 Funding for Projects Advancing Migrant Women’s Democratic Participation Across the European Union
summary: Organizations working to strengthen the voices, leadership, and civic engagement of migrant women across the European Union now have an exciting funding opportunity through the WE-EMPOWER II Grant Programme. Launched by WIDE+ under the European Union Citizens, Equality, Rights and Values Structural Grant (2025–2027), this initiative seeks to support small-scale projects that promote democratic participation, community engagement, advocacy, and leadership among migrant women. With a total funding pool of €80,000 available, the programme is expected to support approximately 15 to 20 impactful projects led by eligible organizations across EU Member States. Application Deadline Deadline for Applications: 6 July 2026 (23:59 CET) Applicants are encouraged to submit their proposals well before the deadline to avoid last-minute technical or administrative challenges. About the WE-EMPOWER II Grant Programme The WE-EMPOWER II project is designed to strengthen the democratic participation of migrant women through targeted grassroots initiatives and collaborative actions. The programme recognizes that democratic participation extends beyond voting and formal political engagement. It includes: Civic engagement Community participation Political involvement Collective learning Advocacy and campaigning Public dialogue Institutional engagement Leadership development The grant programme aims to create opportunities for migrant women to actively influence decisions affecting their lives while strengthening connections between organizations working in migration, gender equality, and social justice. Funding Available The grant programme offers two funding categories: 1. Small-Scale Grants Funding of up to €4,000 Suitable for focused community initiatives and local activities 2. Standard Grants Funding of up to €7,500 Designed for larger projects with broader reach and impact Final grant amounts will be determined during the evaluation process based on the quality, scope, feasibility, and expected impact of each proposal. What Can the Funding Cover? Successful applicants may use grant funds for a wide range of project-related expenses, including: Staff costs Expert and consultant fees Travel expenses Accommodation costs Venue hire Event logistics Project materials and supplies Interpretation services Accessibility-related costs Communication activities Outreach campaigns Visibility and dissemination activities One significant advantage of this funding opportunity is that payments will be made in advance before project implementation begins, helping organizations launch activities without financial barriers. Although co-financing is not mandatory, organizations that can provide additional resources are encouraged to do so. Objectives of the Grant Programme The WE-EMPOWER II grant initiative seeks to achieve several important outcomes, including: Increasing Migrant Women s Participation Projects should contribute to enhancing the participation of migrant women in civic, political, and community spaces. Strengthening Leadership The programme aims to support initiatives that develop leadership skills and create opportunities for migrant women to influence decision-making processes. Improving Visibility Projects should help amplify the voices, experiences, and contributions of migrant women within communities and institutions. Enhancing Collaboration The initiative encourages stronger cooperation among WIDE+ members and the Gender and Migration Working Group. Supporting Grassroots Initiatives The programme seeks to strengthen partnerships between established organizations and migrant-led grassroots movements. Eligible Activities Applicants can propose a wide range of activities that contribute to the programme s objectives. Supported activities include: Capacity-Building and Training Leadership training Skills development workshops Community empowerment programmes Educational sessions Participatory Learning Activities Interactive workshops Learning exchanges Peer-to-peer knowledge sharing Community learning events Awareness-Raising Campaigns Public education initiatives Media campaigns Information dissemination activities Community outreach programmes Advocacy and Community Engagement Policy dialogues Community consultations Advocacy campaigns Stakeholder engagement activities Networking and Knowledge Exchange Networking events Collaborative forums Learning platforms Partnership-building activities Mandatory Project Requirements To be considered eligible, all projects must meet the following requirements: Participation Requirements Include at least one in-person activity Engage a minimum of 20 participants Ensure that at least 50% of participants are migrant women or members of marginalized groups Additional Requirement for Standard Grants Projects applying for standard grants must reach at least 50 participants in total. Deliverables Projects must produce outputs that can be shared within the WIDE+ network, including: Reports Training materials Communication products Learning resources Documentation of project outcomes Meaningful Involvement of Migrant Women A core requirement of the programme is the meaningful participation of migrant women in project implementation. Applications that fail to demonstrate active involvement and leadership by migrant women will not be considered eligible. Eligibility Criteria Organizations applying for funding must meet all of the following requirements: Be a legally registered non-profit organization or association Be based in a European Union Member State Be a WIDE+ member organization or member of the Gender and Migration Working Group Operate mainly at local, regional, or national levels Respect European Union values and principles Comply with Article 2 of the Treaty on European Union Adhere to the European Union Charter of Fundamental Rights Have no conflict of interest regarding the grant call European umbrella organizations are not eligible under this funding opportunity. Selection Process Applications will undergo a two-stage review process. Stage 1: Eligibility Screening Applications will be reviewed to ensure: Administrative compliance Eligibility of applicants Completeness of documentation Adherence to programme requirements Stage 2: Independent Evaluation Eligible proposals will be assessed by a Selection Committee consisting of three independent evaluators. Applications will be scored out of 50 points, and only those achieving at least 30 points will be considered for funding. Evaluation Criteria 1. Alignment with Programme Objectives (15 Points) Evaluators will assess how clearly the project contributes to the objectives and priorities of the grant programme. 2. Quality and Feasibility of Project Design (15 Points) Reviewers will examine: Project structure Methodology Realism of activities Feasibility of implementation Expected outcomes and outputs 3. Intersectional Feminist Approach and Migrant Women’s Empowerment (10 Points) Projects should demonstrate: Intersectional feminist principles Leadership opportunities for migrant women Inclusive approaches for marginalized groups 4. Contribution to the WIDE+ Network (10 Points) Applications will be assessed on their ability to strengthen collaboration, knowledge sharing, and engagement within the WIDE+ network and Gender and Migration Working Group. Important Timeline Call Opens: 6 June 2026 Application Deadline: 6 July 2026 Evaluation Period: July 2026 Results Announcement: July 2026 Contract Signing: July 2026 Project Implementation Period: 1 August – 30 November 2026 How to Apply Applicants must complete the official application form and submit it via email before the application deadline. Applications should be sent to: Email: info@wideplus.org Only complete applications submitted before the deadline will be reviewed. Organizations are advised to carefully review all eligibility requirements and ensure that their proposals clearly demonstrate the meaningful involvement of migrant women, strong project design, and alignment with the objectives of the WE-EMPOWER II programme. This funding opportunity offers a valuable platform for organizations across the European Union to strengthen democratic participation, amplify migrant women s leadership, and contribute to more inclusive and equitable communities. For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Grant Opportunities on OFY here . The post WE-EMPOWER II Grant 2026: Up to €7,500 Funding for Projects Advancing Migrant Women’s Democratic Participation Across the European Union appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Feminist Opportunities Now (FON) Funding 2026: Global Support for Feminist Organizations Advancing Gender Justice and Gender Equality
summary: Feminist organizations around the world continue to play a critical role in advancing gender equality, protecting human rights, and challenging systems that perpetuate discrimination and gender-based violence. However, many of these organizations face significant financial and operational challenges that limit their ability to scale their impact and sustain their advocacy efforts. To address these challenges, Feminist Opportunities Now (FON) has emerged as a transformative initiative dedicated to empowering feminist movements globally. Through funding, capacity strengthening, networking opportunities, and movement-building support, FON is helping feminist organizations build resilience, foster inclusivity, and create lasting social change. Organizations committed to advancing gender justice, eliminating gender-based violence, and promoting equality now have an opportunity to engage with a platform designed specifically to amplify feminist voices and strengthen feminist leadership worldwide. About Feminist Opportunities Now (FON) Feminist Opportunities Now (FON) is a global initiative focused on supporting feminist movements and organizations that are working tirelessly to create a more equitable and inclusive world. The initiative is built around three core principles: Empowering feminist organizations Elevating marginalized voices Advocating for sustainable gender justice FON envisions a future where feminist organizations thrive, where their voices are heard across communities and institutions, and where they have access to the flexible resources needed to adapt, innovate, and overcome challenges. The initiative recognizes that meaningful social change requires strong grassroots organizations, collaborative networks, and sustained investments in feminist leadership. FON s Mission and Vision At the heart of FON s work is a commitment to: Eradicating gender-based violence Advancing gender equality Strengthening feminist movements Promoting inclusivity and diversity Building organizational resilience Supporting sustainable social transformation The initiative seeks to create an environment where feminist organizations can operate effectively, advocate confidently, and influence policies and systems that affect women, girls, and marginalized communities. Funding Opportunities Available Through FON One of the key features of Feminist Opportunities Now is its commitment to providing support that meets the real-world needs of feminist organizations. Unlike traditional funding models that often come with rigid restrictions, FON promotes the importance of flexible funding that allows organizations to: Respond quickly to emerging challenges Adapt strategies to changing environments Strengthen organizational sustainability Expand advocacy and outreach efforts Invest in leadership development Build long-term institutional capacity This flexible approach enables organizations to focus on impact rather than administrative limitations. What Makes FON Unique? 1. Flexible Funding Support FON recognizes that organizations need funding structures that allow them to respond effectively to local realities and evolving challenges. Flexible funding can support: Program implementation Organizational development Advocacy campaigns Community engagement activities Capacity-building initiatives Operational sustainability 2. Capacity Strengthening Beyond financial support, FON invests in strengthening organizations through: Leadership development Institutional capacity building Strategic planning support Organizational resilience initiatives Knowledge sharing and learning opportunities These efforts help organizations become stronger, more sustainable, and better positioned to achieve their goals. 3. Global Feminist Networks FON promotes collaboration and solidarity among feminist organizations across different countries and regions. Participating organizations can benefit from: Cross-border networking opportunities Shared learning experiences Collaborative advocacy efforts Exchange of best practices Movement-building initiatives These connections help amplify collective impact and strengthen feminist movements globally. 4. Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion FON celebrates diversity and seeks to ensure that organizations representing different communities, identities, and experiences are included within its network and support systems. The initiative recognizes that diverse voices are essential for achieving meaningful and sustainable gender justice outcomes. Areas of Focus Organizations working in the following areas may find strong alignment with FON s mission: Gender equality advocacy Women s rights promotion Gender-based violence prevention and response Feminist movement building Youth empowerment Digital rights and online safety Leadership development Community mobilization Social justice initiatives Inclusion and diversity programs Impact of Feminist Opportunities Now FON has demonstrated its commitment to strengthening civil society organizations and feminist movements through: Subgranting support to civil society organizations Capacity strengthening initiatives Promotion of collaborative feminist action Support for grassroots and community-led efforts Through these interventions, the initiative continues to contribute to stronger feminist ecosystems capable of driving meaningful social change. Why Feminist Funding Matters Feminist organizations often work on the frontlines of social transformation. They advocate for marginalized communities, challenge harmful norms, provide essential services, and hold institutions accountable. Investing in these organizations leads to: Stronger communities Improved gender equality outcomes Reduced gender-based violence Increased civic participation More inclusive societies Sustainable development progress By supporting feminist organizations, funding initiatives such as FON help ensure that gender justice movements have the resources and support needed to create lasting impact. How to Apply for FON Funding Organizations interested in exploring available funding opportunities through Feminist Opportunities Now should visit the official FON website to review current funding calls, eligibility requirements, and application procedures. APPLY HERE Applicants are encouraged to regularly monitor the platform for new opportunities, updates, events, and announcements. Deadline No specific application deadline has been publicly stated. Applications may be accepted on a rolling basis depending on available funding opportunities. Interested organizations are encouraged to apply as early as possible and regularly check the official website for updates. Conclusion Feminist Opportunities Now (FON) represents a powerful commitment to strengthening feminist movements worldwide. By combining flexible funding, capacity strengthening, networking opportunities, and a strong focus on gender justice, the initiative is helping organizations overcome barriers and accelerate progress toward equality. Organizations working to eliminate gender-based violence, empower communities, and advance feminist causes should explore the opportunities available through FON and become part of a growing global movement dedicated to lasting social change. For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Grant Opportunities on OFY here . The post Feminist Opportunities Now (FON) Funding 2026: Global Support for Feminist Organizations Advancing Gender Justice and Gender Equality appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Institute for Human Rights and Business Business Engagement Lead Job 2026: Fully Remote Global Opportunity Paying £70,000 Per Year
summary: The Institute for Human Rights and Business (IHRB) is inviting applications for the position of Business Engagement Lead – JUST Stories , a fully remote international consultancy role designed for experienced professionals passionate about climate action, human rights, business transformation, and stakeholder engagement. This unique opportunity places successful candidates at the forefront of one of the most important global conversations of our time: ensuring that climate transitions are not only environmentally effective but also socially inclusive, equitable, and sustainable for workers and communities. The position offers an annual compensation package equivalent to £70,000 per year , provides the flexibility of remote work, and allows professionals from around the world to influence how businesses, investors, governments, and communities navigate real-world climate transitions. About the Institute for Human Rights and Business Founded in 2009, the Institute for Human Rights and Business (IHRB) works to create a more just and regenerative global economy where: Workers and communities can effectively exercise their rights. Businesses integrate respect for people and the environment into their operations and decision-making. Financial institutions leverage their influence to promote responsible business practices. Governments establish policies that encourage rights-respecting and environmentally responsible business conduct. Through research, policy engagement, convening activities, and collaborative initiatives, IHRB seeks to make respect for people and the planet a core part of everyday business operations. Understanding the JUST Stories Initiative The JUST Stories initiative was developed to address a major challenge in climate action implementation. While governments, businesses, and investors increasingly commit to climate goals, many transition efforts fail because workers and communities are often excluded from decision-making processes. Such exclusions can lead to: Community resistance. Project delays. Reputational risks. Financial losses. Poor long-term outcomes. JUST Stories demonstrates that climate transitions become more effective, durable, and investable when workers and communities are actively involved in shaping decisions. Phase I Achievements Between 2024 and 2026, JUST Stories documented real-world examples of people-centered climate transitions, including: Coal transition initiatives in Australia. Solar energy and salt-pan worker transitions in India. Affordable housing transitions in Catalonia. Agricultural transition projects in Brazil. These stories were developed through participatory field research and translated into actionable insights for policymakers, businesses, and investors. Phase II Objectives From 2026 to 2028, the program will expand significantly by producing: 2–3 new stories focused on people-centered decarbonization. 2–3 stories focused on community-led climate adaptation and resilience. The Business Engagement Lead will play a central role in ensuring that lessons from these stories reach influential decision-makers and drive meaningful changes in business practices. About the Business Engagement Lead Role This is not a traditional communications, marketing, or public relations position. Instead, the role focuses on helping senior business leaders, investors, policymakers, and sector experts understand and apply lessons emerging from JUST Stories. The successful candidate will be responsible for developing influence strategies that ensure the stories inform real-world climate transition decisions. Working closely with IHRB s Head of Just Transitions and research teams, the Business Engagement Lead will help translate field-based insights into practical guidance that organizations can implement. Key Responsibilities 1. Develop Influence Strategies The successful candidate will: Identify key decision-makers and stakeholder groups. Map priority audiences across sectors. Analyze transition maturity levels among stakeholders. Design targeted engagement approaches for each story. 2. Create Practitioner-Focused Materials The role involves transforming research findings into practical resources, including: Practitioner briefs. Sector-specific guidance documents. Business-focused learning materials. Decision-making resources for executives and investors. These materials should provide insights on: Governance adaptations. Commercial trade-offs. Decision sequencing. Stakeholder engagement approaches. Transition implementation strategies. 3. Lead Business and Investor Engagement The Business Engagement Lead will: Build relationships with senior corporate leaders. Engage investors and financial institutions. Conduct targeted briefings. Facilitate strategic conversations. Expand IHRB s global private-sector network. 4. Manage Sector Forum Participation Responsibilities include: Identifying high-impact conferences and forums. Positioning JUST Stories contributors at relevant events. Developing engagement materials. Representing IHRB in external discussions. 5. Design and Deliver the Annual Dilemma Forum One of the most important responsibilities will be organizing an invitation-only annual forum involving: Approximately 35–40 senior practitioners. Representatives from 25–30 organizations. Structured peer-learning sessions. Real-world climate transition challenges. Unlike traditional conferences, these forums focus on practical problem-solving and collaborative learning. 6. Develop Digital Masterclass Series The role includes overseeing the creation of educational masterclass content featuring: Climate transition leaders. Business practitioners. Industry experts. These masterclasses will be designed for internal organizational learning and professional development. 7. Influence Monitoring and Learning The successful candidate will establish systems to: Track engagement outcomes. Monitor practitioner responses. Evaluate influence strategies. Assess long-term impact. Support donor reporting requirements. Desired Skills and Experience IHRB is seeking a highly experienced professional who can effectively bridge the worlds of research, climate action, business strategy, and stakeholder engagement. Ideal candidates should demonstrate expertise in: Business Engagement Stakeholder management. Corporate engagement. Executive relationship-building. Strategic influence. Climate and Sustainability Climate transitions. Decarbonization. Adaptation and resilience. Sustainable development. Facilitation and Convening Peer-learning design. Forum facilitation. Multi-stakeholder engagement. Strategic dialogue management. Research Translation Converting research into actionable insights. Producing practitioner-focused content. Knowledge dissemination. Executive communication. Compensation and Benefits The selected candidate will receive: Consultancy fee of £5,833 per month . Equivalent annual compensation of £70,000 . Fully remote work arrangement. International collaboration opportunities. Extensive networking with business, finance, and climate leaders. Opportunity to influence global climate transition practices. Contract running through 29 June 2028 . Work Location This role is fully remote and open internationally. However, applicants must be located in time zones that substantially overlap with Central European Time (CET) or are west of CET to facilitate collaboration with colleagues working across: Europe. Africa. North America. Candidates must: Have legal work authorization in their country of residence. Provide their own laptop and equipment. Maintain reliable internet connectivity. Be willing to travel internationally when required. Important Application Dates Application Deadline 5 July 2026 (11:00 PM UK Time) Interview Schedule First-round interviews: Week commencing 13 July 2026. Second-round interviews: One week later, including a short assessment exercise. Expected Start Date As soon as possible following the recruitment process. Why This Opportunity Stands Out The Business Engagement Lead position offers a rare opportunity to shape how businesses, investors, governments, and communities approach climate transitions on a global scale. By connecting real-world stories with decision-makers, the successful candidate will help ensure that climate action becomes more inclusive, equitable, practical, and sustainable. For professionals seeking to combine expertise in sustainability, human rights, business strategy, and stakeholder engagement, this role represents a highly impactful career opportunity with global reach and long-term significance. APPLY NOW For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Job Opportunities on OFY here . The post Institute for Human Rights and Business Business Engagement Lead Job 2026: Fully Remote Global Opportunity Paying £70,000 Per Year appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: African Union Jobs 2026: Apply for the AU Chairperson’s Youth Envoy (P3) Position
summary: The African Union (AU) is seeking an exceptional youth leader to serve as its next Chairperson s Youth Envoy , a prestigious continental role that will champion youth participation, policy advocacy, and leadership across Africa. Based in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia, this position offers experienced professionals the opportunity to influence Africa s development agenda under Agenda 2063. Applications close on 9 July 2026 . Key Details at a Glance Position Chairperson s Youth Envoy (P3) Organisation African Union Commission (AUC) Location Addis Ababa, Ethiopia Department Women, Gender and Youth Directorate (WGYD) Contract Fixed-term Education Master s + 7 years experience OR Bachelor s + 10 years experience Eligible Age 18–35 years Regions Eligible West, East and Central Africa Salary US$96,921 (International) / US$78,461 (Local) per year Application Deadline 9 July 2026 (11:59 p.m. EAT) ATTENTION!!! Dear Candidates, Thank you for your interest in the African Union. Please complete your profile using the African Union CV template. Be aware that the use of the AU CV template is MANDATORY!!!! Kindly download the CV from the link below, fill and upload to apply for jobs. Download English CV here Download French CV here AU Recruitment Team Job Description AU Values • Respect for Diversity and Team Work • Think Africa Above all • Transparency and Accountability • Integrity and Impartiality • Efficiency and Professionalism • Information and Knowledge Sharing Organization Information Reports to: Cabinet of the Chairperson Directorate/Department/Organ: Women, Gender and Youth WGYD Division: Youth Division Number of Direct Reports: 0 Number of Indirect Reports: 0 Job Grade: P3 Number of Positions: 1 Contract Type: Fixed Term Location: Addis Ababa, Ethiopia Purpose of Job The African Union (AU) Agenda 2063: The Africa We Want, recognizes that the future of Africa is intrinsically linked to the skills, knowledge, innovation, talents and commitment of its young people. The agenda’s aspirations reflect Africa’s collective vision for shared prosperity and well-being, unity and integration, and a continent of empowered citizens with expanded opportunities, where the full potential of women and youth is realized, free from fear, disease, and want. Specifically, Aspiration 6 envisions “an Africa whose development is people-driven, relying on the potential of African people, especially its women and youth, and caring for children.” In line with this vision, AU and its Member States have increasingly prioritized youth development, participation and leadership. Across the continent, most Member States have adopted youth-related policies, strategies, and programmes that recognize both the challenges faced by young people and the immense opportunities they represent for Africa’s transformation. Efforts to strengthen youth participation in governance and decision-making are reflected in the establishment of national youth councils, youth advisory mechanisms, and the appointment of young people to leadership and executive positions. Youth networks and platforms at all levels have also been established to facilitate meaningful youth engagement and to ensure that the perspectives, aspirations and contributions of young people are integrated into policies, strategies and programmes. These mechanisms have contributed significantly to advancing youth inclusion; however, there remains a need for stronger coordination, advocacy, and representation of youth voices within AU processes and across Member States. In recognition of the role young people play in shaping Africa’s development and building on AU’s longstanding commitment to placing young people at the center of the continent’s development agenda, the Chairperson of the African Union Commission (AUC) seeks to appoint the 3rd African Union Commission Chairperson’s Youth Envoy (AUCYE) for the period of a two year term with the possibility of renewing for another term. In close collaboration with WGYD, the Youth Envoy will serve as a dedicated advocate for the interests, aspirations and concerns of Africa’s youth, facilitating dialogue between young people and AU leadership, promoting the implementation of continental youth commitments, and ensuring that youth perspectives inform policies and programmes. Main Functions In the discharge of his/her duties, the Youth Envoy will: Champion AU Youth Policies and Priorities; Amplify Youth Voices and Participation; Mobilize Partnerships and Advocacy for youth development and empowerment; and Serve as a Strategic Liaison between Youth and the AU, including representing African youth voices in AU policy processes. Specific Responsibilities The AUC Chairperson’s Youth Envoy will be responsible for the following functions, among others: Champion AU Youth Policies and Priorities by promoting the implementation of the African Youth Charter, the AU 1 Million Next Level Initiative (1mNL) Initiative, and other relevant AU frameworks and initiatives, while fostering multi-stakeholder partnerships to advance youth development and empowerment. Promote Youth Engagement and Representation by facilitating the meaningful participation of young people, including marginalized and vulnerable groups, in AU policy dialogues, decision-making processes, and continental development initiatives. Strengthen Partnerships and Youth Networks at national, regional, continental, and global levels to amplify youth voices, enhance collaboration, and support the implementation of AU youth commitments. Lead Advocacy and Awareness Efforts to elevate youth issues, promote youth leadership and inclusion, mobilize political commitment, and advocate for increased investment in youth development and youth-centered policies. Support Strategic Youth Platforms and Events by advancing youth participation in key AU-led engagements, including youth dialogues, commemorative events and intergenerational platforms. Advance Youth Inclusion within the AU by advocating for greater youth representation and participation across AU institutions and processes, including initiatives that promote youth leadership and diversity. Coordinate Closely with WGYD to ensure alignment with AU priorities, strengthen institutional collaboration and support the effective delivery of youth development and engagement programmes. Academic Requirements and Relevant Experience Candidates must have a Masters Degree OR a Bachelor’s degree in Development Studies, Public Policy, Political Science, International Relations, Information Technology or a related field with at least seven (7) OR ten (10) years of relevant work experience respectively and three (3) years at expert and/or senior level. Demonstrable understanding of youth empowerment frameworks and initiatives. Proven advocacy and influencing experience, including ability to identify key youth priorities and lead initiatives that drive meaningful social, institutional and policy change. A strong track record in youth mobilization and empowerment, demonstrating the ability to organize, inspire and support young people in advancing transformative change at community, national, regional or international levels. Proven leadership and management capabilities, including the ability to coordinate initiatives, build partnerships, and deliver results in complex and multi-actor environments. Required Skills Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to engage and influence stakeholders at all levels. Sound planning and organizational skills, with the ability to manage competing priorities effectively. Established leadership experience in multi-stakeholder engagement, working effectively with youth networks, governments, private sector actors, media, civil society organizations and development partners. Proven capacity to generate innovative ideas and translate them into practical improvements and results. Diplomatic negotiation skills and ability to build consensus among diverse stakeholders. Ability to work collaboratively in a multi-cultural environment and adapt to changing circumstances. Ability to delegate the appropriate responsibility, accountability, and decision-making authority with regard to performance management and professional development. Demonstrable pan-African youth leadership skills through engagement with AU, UN agencies, and global development platforms. Strong ability to mobilize and coordinate youth networks across Africa for advocacy and development initiatives, including digital advocacy and campaign leadership. Skilled in strategic influence and storytelling, shaping narratives that drive policy attention and action. Ability to connect day-to-day actions with long-term goals, ensuring short-term decisions align with and contribute to broader strategic objectives. Ability to drive innovation by adopting new approaches, taking calculated risks and creating an environment where others feel safe to share ideas and contribute to change. Proficiency in one of the AU working languages (Arabic, English, French, Kiswahili, Portuguese, and Spanish) is mandatory and fluency in another AU language is an added advantage. Leadership Competencies Strategic Insight Change Management Managing Risk Core Competencies Building Relationship Accountable and Complies with Rules ..Learning Orientation Communicating with Influence Functional Competencies Conceptual Thinking Job Knowledge and Information Sharing Drive for Results Continuous Improvement Orientation Notes Candidates must be between the ages of 18-35 for the duration of the appointment. Only candidates who meet all job requirements and are selected for the next stage of the selection process will be contacted. Consideration will be given only to those candidates who have submitted a fully completed online application with an updated curriculum vitae (CV) (not longer than 3 pages), a bio page of national passport or national ID, and the required academic qualifications, such as Diplomas (Bac+2), Bachelor s degrees (Bac+3), as well as names, email addresses and telephone number of three referee Only candidates from the West, East and Central regions are eligible to apply in accordance with geographical rotation. . TENURE OF APPOINTMENT: The Appointment will be made on a fixed term contract for a period of one (1) year, of which the first three (3) months will be considered as a probationary period. Thereafter, the contract may be renewed for a similar period subject to funding availability, satisfactory performance and agreed deliverables GENDER MAINSTREAMING: The AU Commission is an equal opportunity employer and qualified women are strongly encouraged to apply. LANGUAGES: Proficiency in one of the AU working languages (Arabic, English, French, Kiswahili, Portuguese, and Spanish) is mandatory and fluency in another AU language is an added advantage REMUNERATION: The salary attached to the position is an annual lump-sum of US$ 96,921.34 (P3 Step 5) inclusive of all allowances for internationally recruited staff, and US$ 78,461.08 inclusive of all allowances for locally recruited staff of the African Union Commission. -Only candidates who meet all job requirements and are selected for interviews will be contacted. -Consideration will be given only to those candidates who have submitted a fully completed online application with a curriculum vitae (CV), an African passport, and the required academic qualifications, such as Diplomas (Bac+2), Bachelor s degrees (Bac+3), Master s degrees (Bac+5) and any relevant certificate in line with the area of expertise. -The African Union is an equal opportunity employer, and female candidates are strongly encouraged to apply. Requisition ID : 3023 How to Apply Interested candidates must submit their application through the African Union s online recruitment portal before 9 July 2026 (11:59 p.m. East Africa Time) . To complete your application successfully, you will need to: Complete the online application form through the African Union recruitment system. Download and use the mandatory African Union CV template. Applications submitted without the official AU CV template will not be considered. Upload the required supporting documents , including: An updated Curriculum Vitae (maximum three pages) A copy of your national passport or national ID (bio page) Academic certificates and qualifications The names, email addresses and telephone numbers of three professional referees Review your application carefully before submission to ensure all information and documents are complete. Important: Only candidates who meet the eligibility requirements and submit a complete application will be shortlisted for the next stage of the recruitment process. The African Union will only contact candidates selected for interviews. Application Deadline 🗓 Closing Date: 9 July 2026 🕛 Time: 11:59 p.m. East Africa Time (EAT) Candidates are encouraged to submit their applications well before the deadline to avoid last-minute technical issues. Late or incomplete applications will not be considered. CLICK HERE TO FIND OUT MORE AND APPLY HERE The post African Union Jobs 2026: Apply for the AU Chairperson s Youth Envoy (P3) Position appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Youth Endowment Fund Opens Applications for its Youth Advisory Board 2026: Help Shape UK Policies and Influence £200 Million in Youth Violence Prevention Funding
summary: The Youth Endowment Fund (YEF) has officially opened applications for its Youth Advisory Board (YAB) 2026 , offering young people across the United Kingdom an exceptional opportunity to directly influence policies, funding decisions, research, and programmes aimed at preventing violence affecting children and young people. If you are passionate about creating safer communities, amplifying youth voices, influencing government decisions, and ensuring young people s experiences shape national policy, this opportunity provides a meaningful platform to make lasting impact. Application Deadline: Friday, 10 July 2026 About the Youth Endowment Fund The Youth Endowment Fund (YEF) was established in March 2019 by children s charity Impetus with a £200 million endowment from the UK Home Office. Its mission is simple yet ambitious: Prevent children and young people from becoming involved in violence by identifying what works and ensuring evidence informs policy and practice. YEF invests in research, funding programmes, evidence generation, and partnerships that improve outcomes for young people across the UK. A core part of this mission is ensuring young people are not merely consulted but actively involved in decision-making. This is exactly why the Youth Advisory Board exists. What is the Youth Advisory Board (YAB)? The Youth Advisory Board (YAB) is a group of young people who work directly with the Youth Endowment Fund to ensure youth perspectives remain central to every aspect of the organization s work. Rather than making decisions for young people, YEF works alongside them, allowing members to influence strategy, funding priorities, research, communications, governance, and policy engagement. The board primarily meets virtually, making participation more accessible while allowing members from different locations to contribute effectively. What Will Youth Advisory Board Members Do? Successful applicants will play an active role in shaping the work of one of the UK s leading organizations focused on youth violence prevention. Members help YEF in several important ways. 1. Influence Funding Decisions Members contribute to decisions on how nearly £200 million in funding is invested. This includes: Reviewing programmes Interviewing grant applicants Supporting funding assessments Participating in governance boards Working directly with YEF staff 2. Co-Design New Projects Youth Advisory Board members collaborate with staff to develop: New projects Communications campaigns Publications Resources for young people Youth-centred initiatives Their lived experiences help ensure projects genuinely reflect the realities faced by young people. 3. Provide Youth Perspectives Members serve as critical friends to the organization by: Challenging ideas Offering honest feedback Identifying gaps Suggesting improvements Ensuring youth voices remain central 4. Engage with Government and Stakeholders YAB members regularly engage with: Government officials Researchers Education professionals Police representatives Youth justice organizations Health professionals Community organizations They help shape discussions around issues affecting children and young people. 5. Contribute to Research Members participate in research by: Advising research teams Providing lived experience Reviewing findings Helping improve recommendations Their contributions ensure evidence reflects real experiences. Why Join the Youth Advisory Board? Joining YAB provides opportunities far beyond volunteering. Members gain valuable experience in: Leadership Public speaking Policy development Youth advocacy Research Governance Stakeholder engagement Team collaboration Communications National decision-making It is also an excellent opportunity for young people interested in careers in: Public policy Government International development Social work Education Youth justice Research Non-profit leadership Hear from Current Youth Advisory Board Members Current members joined YAB for many reasons, including: Representing underrepresented young people Preventing youth violence Improving education systems Reforming youth justice Supporting safer communities Influencing national policies Ensuring lived experiences inform decision-making Many members draw on personal experiences of exclusion, community violence, youth work, and advocacy to create meaningful change. Youth-Led Projects and Initiatives The Youth Advisory Board contributes to several important initiatives. These include: Yap with YAB Podcast A youth-led podcast where members discuss important issues affecting young people, including: Education Schools Community safety Youth violence Public policy Everyday challenges Moments that Mattered An animated mini-series exploring significant moments that shaped young people s lives and perspectives. The series highlights lived experiences and encourages conversations around youth development and violence prevention. Research Contributions YAB members also contribute to major Youth Endowment Fund publications covering topics such as: Children, Violence and Vulnerability Youth Violence Statistics Racial Disproportionality Education Youth Justice Policing Health Who Should Apply? This opportunity is ideal for young people who: Care about improving young people s lives Want to influence policy Enjoy working with others Believe youth voices should shape decisions Are passionate about reducing violence Want to gain leadership experience Wish to contribute to meaningful national change Previous experience is less important than genuine commitment and enthusiasm. Why This Opportunity Matters Young people understand the realities they face better than anyone else. The Youth Endowment Fund recognizes that sustainable solutions to youth violence must be informed by those with lived experience. By joining the Youth Advisory Board, members become active partners in designing evidence-based solutions that influence funding, policy, and practice across the United Kingdom. Application Deadline Friday, 10 July 2026 Interested applicants should submit their applications before the deadline. For additional information, applicants can review the recruitment brochure and application guidance available through the Youth Endowment Fund website. How to Apply Applicants can: Read the recruitment brochure. Review the application guidance. Complete the application before Friday, 10 July 2026 . Contact the Youth Voice team via youthvoice@youthendowmentfund.org.uk for enquiries Apply Here For more information on this opportunity visit here Discover more global opportunities on OFY, here The post Youth Endowment Fund Opens Applications for its Youth Advisory Board 2026: Help Shape UK Policies and Influence £200 Million in Youth Violence Prevention Funding appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Swiss National Science Foundation (SNSF) Opens New NCCR 6+ (SSH) Call 2026: Major Research Funding Opportunity for Social Sciences and Humanities Researchers
summary: Swiss National Science Foundation Launches Additional NCCR 6+ (SSH) Call for Strategic Long-Term Research Projects The Swiss National Science Foundation (SNSF) has officially opened applications for the National Centres of Competence in Research (NCCR) 6+ Social Sciences and Humanities (SSH) Call 2026 , offering a significant opportunity for researchers to establish long-term interdisciplinary research centres addressing strategic issues in the social sciences and humanities. The call follows the launch of six National Centres of Competence in Research by the Swiss Federal Government earlier this year, with funding reserved for at least one additional NCCR dedicated specifically to Social Sciences and Humanities (SSH) . This prestigious funding programme supports ambitious research collaborations that strengthen Switzerland s research ecosystem while addressing nationally important scientific and societal challenges. Submission Deadline: 1 September 2026 (17:00 CEST) What is the NCCR Programme? The National Centres of Competence in Research (NCCR) programme is one of Switzerland s flagship research funding initiatives administered by the Swiss National Science Foundation (SNSF). Rather than funding individual research projects, NCCRs support: Large-scale collaborative research programmes Long-term interdisciplinary research Multi-university partnerships Strategic national research priorities Capacity building within Swiss higher education The programme enables researchers to build sustainable research networks capable of generating lasting scientific excellence and societal impact. Purpose of the NCCR 6+ (SSH) Call The additional NCCR call specifically targets Social Sciences and Humanities (SSH) . Its objective is to establish new research centres that: Address major challenges within social sciences and humanities. Foster interdisciplinary collaboration. Build stronger research ecosystems. Develop innovative educational and training programmes. Strengthen long-term research priorities within Switzerland. Where proposals include researchers from other academic disciplines, the primary scientific contribution must remain firmly rooted within the Social Sciences and Humanities. Additionally: The majority of participating researchers must be active in SSH disciplines. Projects should clearly demonstrate their contribution to advancing SSH research. Who Can Apply? The SNSF welcomes applications from: Researchers in Social Sciences. Researchers in Humanities. Interdisciplinary research teams with an SSH focus. Researchers who previously submitted proposals during the 6th NCCR Call. New applicants. This inclusive approach allows both returning and first-time applicants to compete for funding. Key Features of the NCCR 6+ (SSH) Call Applicants are encouraged to design research centres that are: 1. Interdisciplinary Projects should integrate expertise from multiple academic fields while maintaining a clear Social Sciences and Humanities foundation. 2. Cross-University Research networks should involve multiple higher education institutions and encourage broad collaboration across Switzerland. 3. Long-Term NCCRs are designed as strategic, long-term research initiatives that strengthen national scientific capacity. 4. Flexible in Size Unlike many large research grants, the SNSF explicitly welcomes: Smaller research consortia Smaller funding requests Shorter-duration NCCRs This creates opportunities for focused, high-impact research teams. Special Requirements for the 6+ SSH Call Applicants should note several additional requirements unique to this funding round. These include: Research centres must be based at a minimum of two home institutions . Financial contributions from participating institutions should correspond appropriately with the requested federal funding. Collaboration among different types of higher education institutions is strongly encouraged. What Makes the NCCR Programme Unique? The NCCR initiative goes beyond research funding. Successful centres contribute to Switzerland s research landscape through: New doctoral and postgraduate training opportunities. Academic networking. Institutional capacity building. Long-term collaboration. Knowledge transfer. Development of sustainable research infrastructures. These investments help position Switzerland as a global leader in strategic research. Selection Process The NCCR selection process consists of multiple rigorous stages. Stage 1: Scientific Evaluation The SNSF evaluates: Outline proposals Full proposals Assessment criteria include: Scientific excellence Research quality Organizational structure Governance Strategic relevance Outstanding proposals are shortlisted for recommendation. Stage 2: Government Assessment Recommended proposals are submitted to the State Secretariat for Education, Research and Innovation (SERI) . SERI conducts: Research policy assessment Higher education policy evaluation Proposal prioritisation Recommendations are then forwarded to the Federal Department of Economic Affairs, Education and Research (EAER) . The EAER is expected to make the final funding decision by the end of 2027 . Research activities are planned to begin on 1 April 2028 . Important Dates Applicants should carefully note the complete timeline. Declaration of Intent Deadline: 1 September 2026 Submission of Outline Proposals Deadline: 20 October 2026 Submission of Full Proposals Deadline: 20 May 2027 Final Funding Decision Expected by the end of 2027 Planned Research Start 1 April 2028 Online Information Event To support applicants, the SNSF will organize an online information session. Date: 8 July 2026 Time: 11:00 a.m. – 12:00 p.m. (Swiss local time) Researchers, universities, and research institutions are encouraged to attend to gain detailed guidance on the application process and eligibility requirements. Why Researchers Should Apply The NCCR programme offers numerous benefits, including: Long-term strategic research funding Opportunities to build interdisciplinary collaborations National recognition Institutional capacity development Development of new research priorities Strong partnerships across Swiss universities Training opportunities for future researchers Lasting scientific impact How to Apply Applicants should carefully review the official call documentation before preparing their submissions. They are also encouraged to register for the online information event and ensure that all required documents are submitted before the relevant deadlines. For additional enquiries, applicants may contact the National Centres of Competence in Research team via: Email: nccr@snf.ch Apply Here Final Thoughts The SNSF National Centres of Competence in Research (NCCR) 6+ Social Sciences and Humanities Call 2026 represents one of Switzerland s premier research funding opportunities for scholars seeking to establish ambitious, collaborative, and long-term research programmes. Researchers working across the Social Sciences and Humanities are strongly encouraged to leverage this opportunity to create impactful research networks that contribute not only to academic excellence but also to Switzerland s future scientific priorities. For more information on this grant opportunity visit here. Discover more grant opportunities on OFY here The post Swiss National Science Foundation (SNSF) Opens New NCCR 6+ (SSH) Call 2026: Major Research Funding Opportunity for Social Sciences and Humanities Researchers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Center for Global Development (CGD) Europe Fellow – Climate Policy & Development 2026: Earn Up to £95,812 While Shaping Global Climate and Development Policy
summary: Apply for the Center for Global Development (CGD) Europe Fellow – Climate Policy Development Position in London The Center for Global Development (CGD) has opened applications for the prestigious Fellow – Climate Policy Development position based in London, United Kingdom . This senior research and policy leadership role offers an exceptional opportunity for experienced professionals working at the intersection of climate change, international development, climate finance, and public policy to influence global development through evidence-based research and high-level policy engagement. With an annual salary ranging from £80,325 to £95,812 , this full-time position is designed for accomplished researchers and policy experts who are passionate about addressing one of the world s greatest challenges—supporting sustainable development in low- and middle-income countries while tackling climate change. Application Deadline: 23 July 2026 at 10:00 BST About the Center for Global Development (CGD) The Center for Global Development (CGD) is an independent, non-partisan, non-profit policy research organization with offices in Washington, D.C. and London (CGD Europe) . Since its establishment in 2001 , CGD has become one of the world s leading development think tanks, producing influential research that shapes international development policies and institutions. The organization collaborates with governments, multilateral institutions, development partners, researchers, and private sector actors to improve policies affecting low- and middle-income countries worldwide. Its work combines: Rigorous empirical research Evidence-based policy recommendations High-level policy engagement Global development advocacy Practical solutions to international development challenges About the Fellow – Climate Policy Development Role The successful Fellow will lead CGD Europe s growing research agenda on climate policy and international development . This role offers the opportunity to: Produce world-class empirical research. Develop practical policy recommendations. Shape international climate policy debates. Engage directly with policymakers. Build partnerships with global institutions. Influence how climate finance supports sustainable development. The Fellow will report directly to Ian Mitchell , Co-Director of the Europe Programme, while collaborating closely with colleagues across both the London and Washington offices. Why This Position Matters Climate change presents one of the defining challenges of our generation. Many developing countries have contributed the least to global greenhouse gas emissions yet face some of the most severe climate impacts. CGD s climate programme seeks to answer critical questions such as: How can climate finance better support developing countries? How should wealthier countries contribute to global climate action? How can carbon markets accelerate sustainable development? What policies encourage green economic growth? How can international climate finance become more accountable and effective? The Fellow will help shape evidence-based solutions to these pressing global issues. Key Research Areas The successful candidate will contribute research across several important areas, including: Climate Finance Research on: Allocation of climate finance Effectiveness of funding Public and private investment Accountability mechanisms Carbon Markets Research may include: Carbon pricing Carbon offsets International carbon trading Market-based climate incentives Green Growth Develop evidence on: Sustainable economic development Climate-compatible growth Green industrial transformation Economic resilience International Climate Policy Support research on: Global climate governance International negotiations Climate responsibilities Policy effectiveness Main Responsibilities The Fellow will take a leadership role across research, policy engagement, fundraising, and mentorship. Key responsibilities include: Research Leadership Design and lead innovative empirical research. Build a coherent climate and development research programme. Produce high-impact publications. Develop policy recommendations. Policy Engagement The Fellow will actively engage with: Government officials International organizations Development partners Researchers Civil society Global climate leaders Activities include: Speaking engagements Expert consultations Conferences Policy dialogues Blogs and opinion pieces Publications The successful candidate will publish: Research papers Policy briefs Reports Working papers Commentary articles These publications will target policymakers, academics, and development practitioners. Resource Mobilisation The Fellow will also: Support fundraising initiatives. Help secure programme funding. Build strategic partnerships. Develop external collaborations. Leadership and Mentoring Additional responsibilities include: Supervising junior researchers. Mentoring mid-level staff. Coordinating programme activities. Representing CGD internationally. Required Qualifications Applicants should possess: A PhD in a relevant field, or A Master s degree combined with significant professional experience. Relevant academic disciplines may include: Climate Policy Economics Environmental Policy International Development Public Policy Climate Finance Political Economy Related social sciences Required Experience Candidates should have: Approximately 12 or more years of policy or research experience. Strong expertise in climate economics, climate finance, or climate policy. Demonstrated success leading complex research projects. Experience conducting quantitative research. Excellent publication record. Experience translating research into practical policy solutions. Required Skills Ideal candidates should demonstrate: Excellent written and verbal communication. Strong organizational skills. Advanced quantitative analysis. Experience using statistical software. Familiarity with AI tools. Ability to work independently. Leadership and stakeholder management skills. Workplace Expectations The successful Fellow is expected to: Be located within commuting distance of London. Work from the London office at least three days per week . Collaborate closely with colleagues across international offices. Application Requirements Applicants must submit: Updated CV Cover Letter Writing Sample (preferably original research) The cover letter should clearly explain how the applicant meets the role requirements. Apply Here Diversity and Equal Opportunity CGD is committed to building an inclusive workplace. Applications are welcomed regardless of: Race Nationality Gender Gender identity Disability Religion Political affiliation Age Sexual orientation Veteran status Marital status The organization values diversity and encourages applicants from all backgrounds. Why You Should Apply This Fellowship provides an exceptional opportunity to: Influence global climate policy. Shape international development debates. Conduct high-impact research. Collaborate with world-leading experts. Build relationships with policymakers. Lead cutting-edge climate research. Mentor the next generation of researchers. Contribute to evidence-based solutions for global climate challenges. Application Deadline Applications close on: 23 July 2026 at 10:00 BST Interested candidates are encouraged to submit their applications well before the deadline. Final Thoughts The Center for Global Development (CGD) Europe Fellow – Climate Policy Development position is one of the most influential climate policy roles currently available for experienced researchers and policy professionals. If you have extensive expertise in climate economics, climate finance, or international development and are passionate about shaping global policy through rigorous research, this opportunity offers an outstanding platform to make a lasting impact on climate and development worldwide. For more information on this job opportunity visit here Discover more job opportunities on OFY here The post Center for Global Development (CGD) Europe Fellow – Climate Policy Development 2026: Earn Up to £95,812 While Shaping Global Climate and Development Policy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Step Forward Fund 2026: National Lottery Grants for Grassroots Organizations in England
summary: The National Lottery Community Fund has officially opened applications for The Step Forward Fund, a groundbreaking and equity-driven grant programme designed specifically to support grassroots organizations led by and serving Global Majority and racially minoritised communities across England. With a total pot of 4 million pounds available and individual awards ranging from 100,000 to 225,000 pounds per organization, this fund represents one of the most significant targeted investments in community-led organizations from racially minoritised backgrounds that England has seen in recent years. This article provides a comprehensive, in-depth guide to The Step Forward Fund what it is, who it is for, how much funding is available, what the money can be used for, how to apply, and what to expect throughout the process. What Is The Step Forward Fund? The Step Forward Fund is a National Lottery Community Fund grant programme that has been co-designed in partnership with The Phoenix Community Trust and their partners. It was developed collaboratively with and for Global Majority and racially minoritised communities, ensuring that the language, design, and priorities of the fund are grounded in the authentic experiences and self-determined needs of the communities it seeks to serve. T he fund has been created to address a specific and well-documented challenge: many grassroots organizations led by Global Majority and racially minoritised communities have successfully received smaller project-based grants, but struggle to make the leap to larger, more sustained investment that would allow them to grow, develop their organizational infrastructure, and deliver long-term impact. The Step Forward Fund is designed to bridge exactly that gap. It provides multi-year funding to help organizations: Continue to advocate for and care for their communities Develop and grow their organizational capacity Sustain and build on the positive impact of their work This fund is part of The National Lottery Community Fund s broader commitment to equity in grant-making specifically its commitment to investing life-changing funding where there is greatest need and putting community power at the heart of how grants are designed and awarded. The National Lottery Community Fund s Commitment to Equity The Step Forward Fund does not exist in isolation. It is one expression of a much deeper institutional commitment from The National Lottery Community Fund to address systemic inequity in the funding landscape. For decades, research and lived experience have consistently shown that grassroots organizations led by Black, Asian, and racially minoritised communities receive disproportionately less funding than their white-led counterparts even when they are delivering equally vital or more impactful work in their communities. The Step Forward Fund directly challenges this imbalance by: Prioritizing organizations that have historically been underserved or excluded from larger funding streams Centering lived experience of structural racism and oppression as a core eligibility criterion Using language co-developed with Global Majority and racially minoritised communities themselves Partnering with The Phoenix Community Trust and their networks to ensure community power is embedded in the fund s design and governance What Is Meant by Global Majority and Racially Minoritised Communities ? The Step Forward Fund uses specific language that has been carefully chosen in consultation with the communities the fund is designed to serve. The term Global Majority and racially minoritised communities includes, but is not limited to, the following groups: Black, African, and Caribbean communities People of any mixed or multiple ethnic background Asian communities Arab communities Jewish communities Gypsy, Roma, and Traveller communities Importantly, this funding is specifically intended for people and organizations with lived experiences of structural racism and oppression. Lived experience in this context refers to knowledge gained through direct, first-hand, and personal experience not simply professional knowledge or academic understanding of these issues. The language used in this fund has been developed through research with these communities and agreed upon with The Phoenix Community Trust and their partners. The National Lottery Community Fund uses this language because the communities themselves are the authority on how they wish to be described and represented. How Much Funding Is Available? The Step Forward Fund has a total of 4 million pounds available to award across England. The funding is structured as follows: Grants are provided at a rate of 50,000 to 75,000 pounds per year Funding periods run for between 2 and 3 years The minimum grant available is 100,000 pounds (50,000 pounds per year for 2 years) The maximum grant available is 225,000 pounds (75,000 pounds per year for 3 years) The National Lottery Community Fund expects to make between 20 and 30 awards in total This multi-year funding model is significant.
